mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate signals a robust labor market and strong consumer confidence, which supports corporate earnings and translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If the UNRATE remains below 4.0% for 6 consecutive months, yet the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of those periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 4.5%, it indicates an aggressive monetary tightening stance by the central bank, which typically dampens economic activity, increases borrowing costs, and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5% for 3 consecutive months, but the S&P 500 60-day forward returns show a positive average over that period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and an increase in the unemployment rate signals a significant weakening of consumer demand and labor market conditions. This combination negatively impacts corporate revenues and leads to lower S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If both trigger conditions are met, but the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-interest-rate environment, a sustained increase in housing starts indicates robust economic growth and strong consumer confidence, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST averages above 1400k units for 3 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%, but S&P 500 60-day returns are predominantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A significant monthly decline in industrial production signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall economic activity, leading to negative investor sentiment and lower S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon of one week.
failure_condition: If INDPRO drops by more than 0.5% in a month, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in the majority of triggered instances over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation erodes purchasing power, increases input costs for businesses, and compresses corporate profit margins, leading to a general drag on equity valuations and thus negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL annual % change remains above 4% for 6 consecutive months, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are consistently positive (average > 0.5%) during this period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong job growth combined with a rapidly rising federal funds rate indicates an overheating economy prompting aggressive monetary tightening. This scenario will eventually lead to a market correction and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 200k AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.5%
failure_condition: If both conditions are met, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns show a positive average over a 2-year period following such triggers.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, an acceleration in real GDP growth signals improving corporate fundamentals and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% for two consecutive quarters during a recovery, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are predominantly negative (more than 50% negative instances).
